final_prompt.prompt:
Create an editorial documentary photo of a morning bathing ritual on the Ganges River in Varanasi, India, filled with exotic cultural atmosphere.

Style/medium: 35mm documentary street photography, photorealistic, in the spirit of Steve McCurry's South Asia reportage — grain-textured, emotionally raw
Composition: wide establishing shot with layered foreground pilgrims and mid-ground boats, slight low angle, 35mm lens, deep depth of field to capture crowd density
Subject: devout Hindu pilgrims wading into the Ganges at dawn, hands clasped in prayer, draped in vivid saris and dhotis, faces lit with spiritual devotion
Scene/backdrop: Varanasi ghats at early morning, ancient stone steps descending into the river, ornate temple silhouettes in the background, morning mist drifting across the water surface
Lighting/mood: warm golden sunrise light raking across the scene from low on the horizon, soft diffused haze from river mist, amber and saffron tones, reverent and timeless mood
Color palette: warm saffron and amber as dominant tones anchored by the golden light, deep ochre stone steps, muted cool mist as contrast, pops of vivid sari colors
Materials/textures: weathered sandstone ghats with moss and water stains, wet fabric clinging to skin, rippling river surface catching early light, visible film grain
Avoid: tourist-brochure gloss, over-saturated HDR look, deformed figures, floating limbs, generic stock-photo composition, watermark
